message:
fix(db): optimize thread message limiting and fix SQLite URL parsing

Thread message queries (queries.rs):
- When a limit is specified, select the newest N messages first (ORDER BY
  created_ts DESC) then reverse to chronological order, instead of loading
  the entire thread and truncating in Rust. For long-running threads with
  thousands of messages this avoids loading and deserializing rows that
  will be immediately discarded.
- Add test: list_thread_messages_limit_returns_latest_window_in_order
  verifying that limit=2 on a 4-message thread returns messages 3 and 4
  in chronological order.

Search v3 backfill (search_v3.rs):
- Replace hand-rolled URL prefix stripping (strip "sqlite:///" etc.) with
  the core crate's sqlite_file_path_from_database_url and
  is_sqlite_memory_database_url helpers, which correctly handle all
  SQLite URL schemes including sqlite+aiosqlite:// and :memory:.
- Update the backfill_url_path_extraction test to cover the new parsing
  and add a sqlite:///:memory: test case.

Messaging tools (messaging.rs):
- Push the 500-message limit into the DB query (Some(500)) instead of
  fetching unlimited rows and draining the Vec in Rust, reducing peak
  memory for threads with thousands of messages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
